facade_colour: warm red brick with lighter trim; substantial 3-storey building

hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: The property has value as a House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Queen Anne Revival and other Victorian and early-twentieth-century architectural styles. Originally built as a private hospital and residence for Dr. Algernon Temple, in 1906, the building was altered and enlarged when it was converted into a senior’s residence known as Bellevue House. Through its construction date and typology, the property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. The property is also associated with the development of the District as a complete community that included residential, institutional, civic, and commercial uses. The property retains many of its original building features, including mansard roofline with dormer windows, two-storey bay window, three-point arched window, flat headed window and door openings, brick cladding with decorative brickwork, and prominent three-storey open porch with pyramidal roof. Contextually, the property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. The property is important in maintaining and supporting the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: warm red brick with lighter trim; substantial 3-storey detached house with projecting balconies at 2nd and 3rd floors
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Well-maintained; large heritage building with intact features; white picket fence
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor: 3 windows (1 in bay, 2 flanking). Second floor: 3 windows with balcony. Third floor: 2 windows with balcony/veranda. Small dormers visible in roof.
photo_observations.door_details: Main entrance with prominent open porch/veranda
photo_observations.porch_details: Full-width veranda at ground level with turned posts. Upper-floor balconies project at 2nd and 3rd floors with decorative railings.
photo_observations.chimney_details: Two chimneys visible: one at each end of roof ridge.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Decorative brickwork; arched windows; three-storey porch; mansard roof with dormers
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: mansard or steep gable with dormers
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Building enlarged for seniors residence (1906); retains historic character; white picket fence
photo_observations.notes: Large detached Victorian house. White picket fence at front. Balconies at upper floors may be later additions but integrate well with the Victorian form.

notes: Large landmark heritage building. Former Dr. Temple hospital / Bellevue House. Mansard roof with dormers, three-storey porch, bay window.

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a gable, consistent with its house-form, detached typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 3, 3, 2 window bays from lower to upper levels, using mixed, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include bay window, decorative brick, original windows, cornice, string courses.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: The property retains many of its original building features, including mansard roofline with dormer windows, two-storey bay window, three-point arched window, flat headed window and door openings, brick cladding with decorative brickwork, and prominent three-storey open porch with pyramidal roof. facade_description: Row three-storey house-form in warm red brick with lighter trim; substantial 3-storey building with a gable roof. Features include a double-height projecting bay, decorative bargeboards, string courses and a decorative cornice. Building enlarged for seniors residence (1906); retains historic character; white picket fence. Constructed 1890-1903.
